page.title: Multiple Myeloma — RepurpOS | OpenSourceMedicine
page.description: Structured alterations and therapeutics for Multiple Myeloma: molecular targets, clinical biomarkers, phenotypes, and ranked drugs from Open Targets, HPO, ChEMBL, and DGIdb.
page.hero: Disease intelligence for Multiple Myeloma: 155 alterations and 764 ranked therapeutics from curated public databases.

gmi_articles.title: Curcumin has potential in the treatment of plasma cell dyscrasias.
Administered curcumin to a patient with laryngeal amyloidosis and smoldering myeloma and found that the patient has shown a lack of progression of his disease for a period of five years.

disclaimer: Alterations and therapeutics are aggregated from public databases (Open Targets, HPO, ChEMBL, DGIdb, and others). Natural agents are candidates from OSMF narrative reviews on opensourcemed.info/chronic-disease.html — not approved treatments. This page is for research orientation only — not medical advice.

clinical_trials.title: Curcumin and Piperine in Patients on Surveillance for Monoclonal Gammopathy, Smoldering Myeloma or Prostate Cancer
A Study Comparing a Plant-Based Diet With Supplements and Placebo in People With Monoclonal Gammopathy of Undetermined Significance (MGUS) or Smoldering Multiple Myeloma (SMM)
Efficacy of NF-kB Inhibition for Reducing Symptoms During Maintenance Therapy in Multiple Myeloma Patients

Curcumin (Diferuloylmethane Derivative) With or Without Bioperine in Patients With Multiple Myeloma
Study of the Safety and Usefulness of Liposomal Curcumin in Multiple Myeloma

remission.spontaneous_remission_rate: Essentially zero; MGUS (precursor) progresses to myeloma in 1%/year; median survival without treatment <1 year once symptomatic
remission.best_intervention_remission_rate: Daratumumab + VRd (daratumumab + bortezomib + lenalidomide + dexamethasone) induction + autologous SCT: 66% MRD-negative at 24 months (PERSEUS); CAR-T (idecabtagene vicleucel, ciltacabtagene autoleucel): 71-75% overall response, 28-33% CR in heavily pretreated patients; 5-year survival now ~55% (vs ~20% in 2000)
remission.gap_size: Moderate — outcomes dramatically improved but cure remains elusive; CAR-T access critical unmet need
remission.barrier_detail: BCMA-targeted CAR-T therapy is transformative but manufacturing delays (6+ weeks), $420K+ cost, and limited centre access restrict use; bispecific antibodies (teclistamab) offer alternative; a functional cure remains an aspiration not a reality for most patients
remission.last_soc_change: 2024 — talquetamab (GPRC5D bispecific) approved; 2022 ciltacabtagene autoleucel CAR-T approved; 2021 idecabtagene vicleucel CAR-T first-ever for MM; 2019 daratumumab as frontline; bortezomib/lenalidomide backbone since 2003-2006
remission.outdated_treatments: Melphalan + prednisone as first-line in transplant-eligible patients (triplet/quadruplet regimens clearly superior); thalidomide in the era of lenalidomide (more side effects, equivalent efficacy); bisphosphonate without RANKL inhibitor discussion in bone disease
remission.adjunctive_emerging: Exercise during myeloma therapy (MASCOT trial — reduces fatigue, increases OS signal); neridronate/zoledronic acid for bone protection; gut microbiome optimisation for IMiD response prediction; vitamin D for IMID-related bone metabolism
remission.frontier_signal: BCMA/CD3 bispecific antibodies (teclistamab, elranatamab — off-the-shelf monthly subcutaneous); GPRC5D targeting; tandem CAR constructs; CAR-T as frontline (MonumenTAL, KarMMa-3 Phase 3 early line); anti-CD38 + BCMA combinations; minimal residual disease (MRD)-guided consolidation
remission.notes: Manually curated from landmark trials, registries, and guidelines (disease_db_100.json). Protected from automated overwrite.
